# vanta_vendor_finding (Resource)

A finding attached to a vendor. `remediation` is only meaningful when `risk_status` is `REMEDIATE`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "vanta_vendor" "acme" {
  name = "Acme Inc"
}

resource "vanta_vendor_finding" "pentest" {
  vendor_id   = vanta_vendor.acme.id
  content     = "Vendor has not performed a penetration test in the past 15 months."
  risk_status = "REMEDIATE"

  remediation = {
    state             = "OPEN"
    requirement_notes = "Request an updated penetration test report."
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `content` (String) The content of the finding.
- `risk_status` (String) Risk status. One of `ACCEPT`, `REMEDIATE`, `NONE`.
- `vendor_id` (String) ID of the vendor this finding belongs to. Changing this forces replacement.

### Optional

- `document_id` (String) ID of a document to link. Changing this forces replacement.
- `remediation` (Attributes) Remediation details. Populate when `risk_status` is `REMEDIATE`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--remediation))
- `security_review_id` (String) ID of a security review to link. Changing this forces replacement.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Finding ID.

<a id="nestedatt--remediation"></a>
### Nested Schema for `remediation`

Required:

- `state` (String) Remediation state. One of `OPEN`, `CLOSED`.

Optional:

- `requirement_notes` (String) Notes describing what is required to remediate the finding.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Vendor findings are imported with a composite "<vendor_id>/<finding_id>" ID.
terraform import vanta_vendor_finding.pentest 66bb83dc14f5709efe418859/66bb83e06c54dc42afedb174
```
